site.title: AtomicFlix
site.description: A niche streaming home for classic science-fiction serials, one-off adaptations, and cosmic curiosities.

hero.headline: Binge the Golden Age
hero.subheadline: Classic futures, restored and re-serialized for modern screens.

featured.label: The Faith of Generative Reality
featured.reason: A new spiritual vision for an age of black holes, consciousness, cosmic recursion, and generative reality

series.title: The City at World’s End
series.author: Edmond Hamilton
series.description: A quiet American town is hurled into the unimaginable far future, where the last ruins of civilization stand against cosmic desolation.
series.longDescription: AtomicFlix presents a serialized adaptation of Edmond Hamilton’s sweeping end-of-time adventure, blending mystery, wonder, and stark cosmic scale.

chapters.title: Cataclysm!
chapters.description: A familiar town wakes into an alien stillness and the first signs emerge that the world itself is no longer the one it knew.

chapters.title: The Incredible
chapters.description: The truth widens as the town’s survivors discover they have not merely been moved, but cast into an age beyond imagination.

chapters.title: Dying Planet
chapters.description: The survivors confront the desolation of a world at its end, where the last remnants of humanity cling to existence against the encroaching void.

chapters.title: Dead City
chapters.description: Keniston and Hubble journey out beyond the edge of town, into the unknown dead earth beyond.

chapters.title: In the Red Dawn
chapters.description: The first morning after the cataclysm, the town has froze in place and the people are starting to panic.

chapters.title: Caravan into Tomorrow
chapters.description: The survivors set out on a desperate journey across the dead earth, seeking refuge in the distant city that may hold the key to their survival.

chapters.title: Under the Dome
chapters.description: The survivors find themselves trapped under a mysterious dome, where they must confront the strange forces that govern their new world and fight for their survival.

series.title: Two Thousand Miles Below
series.author: Charles Willard Diffin
series.description: An expedition into the unimaginable depths uncovers a hidden world beneath the earth.
series.longDescription: A subterranean serial of peril, discovery, and classic magazine-era wonder.

chapters.title: Descent
chapters.description: A dangerous mission begins beneath the surface of the known world.

chapters.title: The Hidden Vaults
chapters.description: The explorers encounter signs that the deep earth is neither empty nor dead.

chapters.title: Kingdom Under Stone
chapters.description: What seemed like geology becomes a living realm with its own power and peril.

series.title: First on the Moon
series.author: Jeff Sutton
series.description: A pioneering lunar mission becomes a tense race between courage, mystery, and the unknown.
series.longDescription: A sleek atomic-age journey to the moon, serialized for modern binge watching.

chapters.title: Countdown
chapters.description: A daring mission leaves Earth behind and enters the silence beyond the atmosphere.

chapters.title: Transit
chapters.description: The crew faces mechanical strain, psychological pressure, and the growing presence of lunar mystery.

chapters.title: The Silent Surface
chapters.description: The moon is reached, but the first footsteps reveal more questions than triumph.

blitz.title: House on the Borderland
blitz.creator: Guest Feature
blitz.description: A striking one-off adaptation of cosmic dread and visionary horror.

blitz.title: Cosmonascence
blitz.creator: AtomicFlix
blitz.description: A cosmic meditation on emergence, generative reality, and the birthward movement of existence.

blitz.title: Speed of Light
blitz.creator: AtomicFlix
blitz.description: A visual explainer exploring c, relativity, and the strange discipline of cosmic speed.

blitz.title: On Christ
blitz.creator: AtomicFlix
blitz.description: A cosmic meditation on generative reality through the lens of Christ as a cosmic figure.

blitz.title: Courage The Cowardly Dog - The Vampire Muriel Mystery
blitz.creator: Cartoon Network
blitz.description: Surreal storytelling of Courage the Cowardly Dog, the enigmatic 'Vampire Muriel' episode in the public domain

contributors.title: Contributors
contributors.intro: AtomicFlix welcomes collaborators who love classic science fiction and want to help bring overlooked stories to modern screens.
techstack: Text Editing - My 1997 TextPad (License still valid!)
AI Video Generation and character design - Flicki.com
Poster Art and Thumbnails - Windows Snipping Tool, the attached gen.py script
Web and CDN/S3 Hosting - Digital Ocean
workflow: Think of a book that inspired me when I was poor and suffering and riding the bus home from work.
Download the latest source text from Project Gutenberg
Edit the chapters using My 1997 Textpad
Generate AI video and character designs using Flicki.com
Redfine the characters each time for some reason
Remove the background music layers because they are too loud
Edit the chapters using VideoProc VLogger and increase the speed of the cuts
Export the chapters as MP4s and upload to Digital Ocean
Create poster art and thumbnails using Windows Snipping Tool and the attached gen.py script
Manually edit the JSON files to add the chapters to the site
Watch my own videos and notice all of the mistakes I made

contact.message: Send us a brief note about what you’d like to help with, along with examples of your work if available.

contributors.notes: A formal contributor workflow may be added later as the site grows.

suggestBook.title: Suggest a Book
suggestBook.intro: Know a forgotten sci-fi novel or public-domain gem that deserves the AtomicFlix treatment? We’d love to hear about it.
guidelines: Explain why it would make a strong adaptation
Provide a source-text link if available
Share any visual or tonal inspiration

contact.message: We’re especially interested in classic science fiction, weird fiction, speculative adventure, and visually striking forgotten works.

suggestBook.notes: Please include as much sourcing information as you can.
